architecture_summary: The paper introduces the Banzhaf and Banzhaf-Owen values as novel game-theoretical centrality measures for ranking terrorists in a network, integrating the complete topology of the network together with node weights, edge weights, and an a priori coalitional structure. It applies sampling-based approximation algorithms to the Zerkani network and compares the resulting rankings with those from the Shapley value and the Owen value.

novel_contributions: introduces the Banzhaf and Banzhaf-Owen values as novel centrality measures for ranking terrorists in a network
provides approximation algorithms and the corresponding R-codes for both centrality measures
applies the Banzhaf and Banzhaf-Owen values to rank the members of the Zerkani network
compares the rankings obtained with Banzhaf and Banzhaf-Owen against the Shapley value and the Owen value
uses a partition with ten unions to incorporate coalitional structure into the ranking

key_claims.claim: The paper introduces the Banzhaf and Banzhaf-Owen values as novel centrality measures for ranking terrorists in a network, integrating the complete topology of the network and a coalitional structure on the nodes.
The paper provides approximation algorithms and the corresponding R-codes for both centrality measures.
The members of the Zerkani network are ranked using the Banzhaf and Banzhaf-Owen values, and the results are compared with rankings obtained from the Shapley value and the Owen value.
The Zerkani network contains 47 members, so approximation methodologies are required for the Banzhaf value and the Banzhaf-Owen value.
The paper states that the main novelty lies in the fact that the Banzhaf-Owen value was never used to analyse a terrorist network.

For the monotonic weighted connectivity TU-game, the top-ranked terrorist under the estimated Banzhaf value is Abdelhamid Abaaoud with 38.326053, while under the estimated Banzhaf-Owen value the top-ranked terrorist is Khalid Zerkani with 39.498143.
key_claims.evidence_section: The Banzhaf value approximation in Zerkani network / The Banzhaf-Owen value approximation in Zerkan network

key_claims.claim: The paper reports that the Owen value and the Banzhaf-Owen value estimations show smaller inequalities between components than the corresponding Shapley and Banzhaf value estimations for $(N, v^{muconn})$.
key_claims.evidence_section: Comparison with the usage of the Shapley value and the Owen value
